perturbation.original_text: Every person who has been given access to a University of Nebraska information system or who has access to or knowledge of confidential or sensitive information or data, is obligated to keep such data confidential .
perturbation.changed_text: Every person who has been given access to a University of Nebraska information system or who has access to or knowledge of confidential or sensitive information or data, is encouraged to keep such data confidential, but isn't strictly obligated.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the obligation to maintain confidentiality from 'is obligated' to 'is encouraged, but isn't strictly obligated'. This contradicts the later sections that assume strict adherence to confidentiality policies and threaten consequences for breaches.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I understand that misuse of confidential or sensitive information or data, whether intentional or due to neglect on my part, is a breach of policy and grounds for corrective action which may include my dismissal , the termination of access to University resources, or the termination of a contractual agreement and may subject me to possible civil and/or criminal legal action .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original 'is obligated' to 'is encouraged, but isn't strictly obligated.' The contradicted text states that 'misuse of confidential or sensitive information or data...is a breach of policy and grounds for corrective action.' The change directly contradicts the consequence section that assumes a strict obligation for maintaining confidentiality. By weakening the obligation, the enforcement of penalties outlined in the contradicted section becomes questionable, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: I will only access confidential or sensitive data for legitimate business purposes as permitted by the University and will not use confidential or sensitive data for personal use .
perturbation.changed_text: I will only access confidential or sensitive data for legitimate business purposes as permitted by the University and may use confidential or sensitive data for personal use so long as the data has been sufficiently anonymized.
perturbation.explanation: This change allows for the use of anonymized confidential data for personal use. This directly contradicts the original statement and creates a contradiction with the agreement's overall aim to prevent any unauthorized use of sensitive data, regardless of anonymization, creating an in-text contradiction with the rest of the non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In all instances as described above, I acknowledge my personal obligation to protect, through execution of reasonable steps, the confidentialit y, integrity and privacy of this data in accordance with University policies, regulatory requirements or other special handling requirements, and prevent unauthorized access, modification, destruction or disclosure of sensitive or confidential data . In the absence of stated guidance or without a clear indication of sensitivity, I will treat and handle all data as if it were sensitive.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits using confidential or sensitive data for personal use. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing personal use of anonymized data. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which mandates protecting the confidentiality, integrity, and privacy of data, and preventing unauthorized access or disclosure, regardless of whether the data is considered sensitive. The introduction of the exception makes it unclear which rule takes precedence, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: I will maintain the conﬁdent iality of this information and if uncertain about the appropriateness of who this information may be shared with in the course of my authorized access , I will discuss any questions I have with my supervisor or the Central Administration security o ﬃce.
perturbation.changed_text: I will maintain the conﬁdent iality of this information unless directed otherwise by a supervisor, regardless of the appropriateness of the disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an exception to the confidentiality agreement, allowing a supervisor to override the confidentiality requirement. This change introduces ambiguity by potentially conflicting with the overall confidentiality requirement, because of this change, there is an in-text contradiction in the document if the supervisor tells the employee to breach confidentiality
perturbation.contradicted_text: Every person who has been given access to a University of Nebraska information system or who has access to or knowledge of confidential or sensitive information or data, is obligated to keep such data confidential . Accordingly, every University of Nebraska employee, student, volunteer, contractor, business associate, or guest who either has or will be given access to printed or online confidential data is required to read and acknowledge his or her understanding of this agreement before being granted access .
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows a supervisor to override the confidentiality requirement, directly contradicting the contradicted text which states that every person with access to confidential information is obligated to keep it confidential. This creates a clear and direct conflict, as the supervisor's directive permits a breach of confidentiality that the contradicted text explicitly forbids. The change introduces ambiguity and undermines the overall confidentiality agreement, fitting the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: I will not share m y computer user login IDs and passwords with anyone, at any time, for any reason, except in cases necessary to facilitate computer maintenance and repairs.
perturbation.changed_text: I will not share my computer user login IDs and passwords with anyone, at any time, for any reason, except in cases necessary to facilitate computer maintenance and repairs or with explicit permission from my supervisor for collaborative work.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause allows sharing of login credentials with supervisory permission, contradicting the initial absolute prohibition. This creates ambiguity regarding the security protocols and an in-text contradiction as it increases the risk of unauthorized access.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I will contact the University immediately if I discover that my computer user login ID or password has been compromised, if I suspect that University digital asset ha s been misused, or if I suspect that someone has obtained unauthorized access to confidential or sensitive information or data on the University of Nebraska information system .
perturbation.justification: The original text has a strict rule about not sharing login credentials. The changed text introduces an exception: sharing with supervisory permission. The contradicted text requires immediate notification if credentials have been compromised. Because credentials are now shared according to the changed text, the risk of compromise increases, which makes the initial 'no sharing' rule contradict the 'compromised credentials' section, thus it's a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: I will not disclose any budget, financial, personnel, payroll or procurement information except to the extent necessary to carry out the responsib ilities of my employment or as otherwise required by the law.
perturbation.changed_text: I will not disclose any budget, financial, personnel, payroll or procurement information except to the extent necessary to carry out the responsib ilities of my employment or as otherwise required by the law, or if such disclosure benefits the University's public image.
perturbation.explanation: This adds 'or if such disclosure benefits the University's public image' introduces a subjective and potentially conflicting exception. What constitutes 'benefit' is undefined and could lead to breaches of confidentiality under the guise of public relations. Because of the inserted clause the employee can potentially breach the confidentiality of the financial data in order to improve the university's image, creating an in-text contradiction with the aim of the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Every person who has been given access to a University of Nebraska information system or who has access to or knowledge of confidential or sensitive information or data, is obligated to keep such data confidential . Accordingly, every University of Nebraska employee, student, volunteer, contractor, business associate, or guest who either has or will be given access to printed or online confidential data is required to read and acknowledge his or her understanding of this agreement before being granted access .
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the confidentiality obligation ('or if such disclosure benefits the University's public image'). This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that everyone with access to confidential information 'is obligated to keep such data confidential.' The added clause allows for potential breaches of confidentiality based on a subjective assessment of 'benefit' to the University's public image, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: I will contact the University immediately if I discover that my computer user login ID or password has been compromised, if I suspect that University digital asset ha s been misused, or if I suspect that someone has obtained unauthorized access to confidential or sensitive information or data on the University of Nebraska information system .
perturbation.changed_text: I will contact the University within a reasonable timeframe if I discover that my computer user login ID or password has been compromised, if I suspect that University digital asset ha s been misused, or if I suspect that someone has obtained unauthorized access to confidential or sensitive information or data on the University of Nebraska information system .
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'immediately' with 'within a reasonable timeframe' creates ambiguity regarding the urgency of reporting security breaches. The original text requires immediate action, while the new text allows for delayed reporting. This weakens security protocols, because of the new change on when to report security breaches, and it creates an in-text contradiction with other parts of the agreement where the employee has to take 'reasonable steps' when protecting confidential data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In all instances as described above, I acknowledge my personal obligation to protect, through execution of reasonable steps, the confidentialit y, integrity and privacy of this data in accordance with University policies, regulatory requirements or other special handling requirements, and prevent unauthorized access, modification, destruction or disclosure of sensitive or confidential data . In the absence of stated guidance or without a clear indication of sensitivity, I will treat and handle all data as if it were sensitive.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires immediate reporting of security breaches. The changed text replaces 'immediately' with 'within a reasonable timeframe'. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which obligates the user to protect data through 'execution of reasonable steps'. The immediate reporting requirement is a reasonable step, but now 'reasonable timeframe' is ambiguous, weakening the obligation and creating a conflict on what the reasonable step is for reporting security breaches. Thus, the change creates an ambiguity regarding the urgency and what reasonable steps consist of in the contradicted text.
